name: RABBIT-FEATURE-BUG-1
title: Step 4 bypass warning uses plain [rabbit] prefix instead of [🐇 rabbit 🐇] brand string
description:
The rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md instructs the dispatcher (main session LLM) to emit a Step 4 bypass warning prefixed with plain text '[rabbit]':

  [rabbit] Step 4 SKIPPED: .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker active. Run /rabbit-config human-approval true to restore the gate and require approval again.

The canonical rabbit brand prefix per tdd-subagent Inv 5 (and contract Inv 35/36 about rabbit_print) is the emoji-framed form '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]'. tdd-step.py source is forbidden from using the plain '[rabbit]' literal outside of imports/comments precisely to enforce this. LLM-emitted operational messages should follow the same brand convention so users see one consistent prefix across script output and dispatcher output.

Same drift exists in tdd-subagent Inv 24 (describing the dispatch-tdd-subagent.py prompt preamble note) and in the actual dispatch script's yellow-coloured preamble note. Both use '[rabbit]' plain prefix.

Fix: update the SKILL.md warning text to use '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]' brand string (cosmetic edit; no behaviour change). Update the dispatch script preamble note the same way. Also amend tdd-subagent Inv 24 wording so the spec doesn't sanction the wrong prefix.

Reproduce: invoke rabbit-feature-touch with .rabbit-human-approval-bypass marker present; observe the dispatcher's Step 4 message uses '[rabbit]' instead of '[🐇 rabbit 🐇]'.

history.note: Temporarily closed before CONTRACT-BACKLOG-19 housekeeping wave to prevent accidental closure by wave b/b filings; will be reopened after the wave completes

Scope under-captured: fix spans two features (rabbit-feature and tdd-subagent), each with distinct files. Closing and refiling as two sibling bugs (one per feature) to align with rabbit-feature-touch single-feature-per-touch contract. See sibling RABBIT-FEATURE-BUG-N and TDD-SUBAGENT-BUG-N.
